title: AI in healthcare: hype vs. reality in 2025
post:
The healthcare AI space is a minefield of overpromising. Vendors claim miraculous accuracy. Hospitals are cautious. And the actual reality is somewhere in the messy middle.

Where AI is genuinely working: imaging analysis, drug discovery, administrative automation, and patient monitoring. Where it's struggling: diagnosis in ambiguous cases, understanding patient context, and handling the unexpected. The difference is whether there's sufficient clean data and well-defined parameters.

My advice: be skeptical of both the hype and the backlash. Look at specific use cases, specific data, specific results. Healthcare AI isn't magic and it isn't a threat — it's a tool that works when applied correctly.

Where have you seen AI work well (or poorly) in healthcare? Thoughts?
